Empires & Puzzles Raleigh Review: The Slow-Burn Sage of the Stag

Raleigh brings a unique mix of boosted health and mana acceleration to the 4-star arena. But with Slow speed and aging stats, is he a hidden gem for Rush wars or just a dusty tome on the shelf? We break down the math.
Raleigh Review: Is This 4-Star Druid Worth The Paper He's Printed On?
Raleigh: The "Heimdall Junior" Who Forgot His Coffee
Raleigh enters the arena as a

of the Clash of Knights event. Released back in early 2024, he was designed as a support powerhouse, packing a toolkit that reads like a greatest hits album of 5-star abilities: Boosted Health, Attack Buffs, and the holy grail—Mana Generation.
However, there's a catch (there's always a catch). He moves at the speed of a sloth wading through molasses. As a

hero in a world that keeps getting faster, Raleigh occupies a strange space. He's a "win-more" hero who can make your team unstoppable... if he ever charges. He’s essentially the 4-star, budget-friendly cousin of Heimdall, minus the revival lottery ticket.

Raleigh’s design screams "scholarly druid who spends more time in the library than the gym." He’s depicted with a magnificent stag-horned headdress and a falcon perched on his hand, suggesting he’s one with nature—or at least really good at bird watching. The open tome in his hand (The Tome of the Stag, presumably) adds to his mystical, learned vibe. He looks like the kind of guy who would scold you for walking on the grass while simultaneously healing your sprained ankle.
Raleigh’s stats tell the story of a hero from a different era.
In the harsh light of 2026, Raleigh’s base stats are undeniably
, he lags significantly behind modern 4-star juggernauts (like the Beowulf heroes) who regularly sport 800+ in both stats. His

is decent but no longer impressive. To make him survive long enough to cast his Slow special in today's meta, you will almost certainly need to Limit Break him (LB1 and likely LB2) and emblem him heavily for bulk. Without those investments, he’s liable to crumble before he can open his book.
Special Skill: Tome of the Stag
is a loaded skill, theoretically offering everything a team needs to snowball to victory.

This is his bread and butter. 500 HP is a significant chunk, especially at the 4-star level. The ability to exceed max HP (Overheal) is critical for countering Max Health reduction and surviving heavy hits.
A solid damage boost. While it starts modest, it turns your team into hard-hitters by the end of the duration.
This is the crown jewel. +24% is a massive boost, helping Average speed heroes charge in 9 tiles and Slow heroes in roughly 9-10 tiles (depending on troops).
. By the time Raleigh fires, the battle’s momentum has often already swung. If you’re winning, he seals the deal. If you’re losing, he might not fire in time to save you.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Stag family offers some interesting utility, specifically geared towards countering Attack Down ailments.

is fantastic if you are facing heroes that reduce attack (like Scarlett or various Barbarians). Instead of getting weaker, your heroes get a massive Attack Buff (+40%/+60%/+70%). It’s a niche counter, but hilarious when it works.

This is a standard passive for this family. Gaining 5% health when receiving a buff adds a layer of survivability, especially if paired with other buffers. It helps Raleigh stay alive while waiting for those 12 tiles.
fits his kit perfectly. Since he's a slow healer, he is often the target of snipers trying to take him out before he fires. Passive regeneration helps mitigate chip damage and keeps him in the fight.
Upon release, Raleigh was hailed as a "Mini-Heimdall" and a staple for Rush Tournaments. He offered a rare source of Green Boosted Health at the 4-star level.

The game has sped up immensely. Average is the new Slow, and Fast is the baseline. Raleigh’s stats have fallen behind, making him squishy. His skills are still impactful, but the delivery system (Slow mana) is archaic outside of specific modes.
. He is not a general-purpose healer. You will not take him into a standard Diamond Raid or a War attack unless you are desperate. His home is almost exclusively
Raleigh needs to survive. There is no other priority. If he dies with a full mana bar, he has failed.
Maximizes his survivability. With his older stats, he needs every point of Defense and HP to survive modern snipers.
His tile damage will be negligible.
Slightly higher tile damage and stronger DoT (if he had any, which he doesn't).

path is the only logical choice. You are bringing him to cast his spell, not to whack enemies with his book.
One of the best mechanics in the game, vital for Towers and hard PvE stages.
+24% is a top-tier buff that can drastically change the flow of battle.
In Very Fast modes, his Slow speed drawback vanishes, making him an absolute beast.
Slow mana is a death sentence in many modern match-ups.
Unlike Rigard or Ptolemy, he doesn't clean status ailments, leaving your team vulnerable to DoT or defense down.
This is his kingdom. He fires in 6 tiles and makes your team unkillable while speeding them up even more.
The Boosted Health is crucial for offsetting the permanent damage from curses.
In long boss fights, his mana gen and overheal allow for a safe, controlled victory.
Raleigh shines when he can act as the engine for a slower, grindier team, or when the game mode mitigates his speed.
Li Xiu (Costume) - Raleigh - Franz - Colen (Costume) - Caedmon

In Rush, Raleigh keeps the team overhealed. Franz increases damage taken. Colen nukes. Li Xiu controls mana. Raleigh's mana buff helps even in Rush by reducing the tile breakpoint slightly for others or countering mana slows.
Raleigh - Gullinbursti - Proteus - Wilcox - Damage Dealer
Double Overheal (Raleigh + Gullinbursti) ensures your team effectively has double HP bars.
shut him down completely by blocking buffs or dispelling them.
heroes (like Miki or Peters) can stop him from ever firing.
Do not pair him with other Slow heroes in standard speed modes unless you have a mana booster like Ludwig or Xnolphod to get them started.

If you pull Raleigh early, he is a great asset for completing the Map (Season 1 & 2). His overheal will let you punch above your weight class. However, don't prioritize him over essential Average speed healers like
for general play. Level him for your Rush Tournament roster.

You likely have him benched. He’s a "break glass in case of Rush Tournament" hero. If you are competitive in 4-star tournaments, he is worth keeping and maybe even Limit Breaking just for those specific weeks. Otherwise, save your resources for the latest Construct or Astral heroes.
The community has always had a soft spot for Raleigh, mostly because of his similarity to 5-star heavyweights.
"It’s a 4-star Heimdall with mana generation increase... a usefull Hero."
Others focused on his specific utility:
"Mana, mana, mana. Anything that boosts mana... is a big deal."
However, the speed limit is a common complaint:
"His mana boost skill is useless during rush wars / tournaments , right?"
(It's not useless, but diminishing returns apply).

Raleigh is like a classic hardcover book in an age of Kindles—charming, weighty, and reliable, but not exactly built for speed. He offers a powerful, cohesive skillset that is unfortunately shackled by his Slow mana speed and aging stat line. He remains a
choice for Rush Tournaments and specific PvE challenges, but for everyday raiding, you’re better off leaving this sage in the emerald forest.
A specialized tool for the Rush meta, but too slow for the daily race.
A comprehensive review of Raleigh, the 4-star Nature Druid from the Stag Family in Empires & Puzzles. We analyze his boosted health, mana generation skills, and viability in the modern meta.
